zotero.abstractNote: Density estimation is a fundamental task in machine learning. While many models exist, choosing the right one for a given dataset is often a manual, trial-and-error process. We propose Automatic Bayesian Density Analysis (ABDA), a framework that automates the selection and training of density estimators. ABDA leverages the properties of Sum-Product Networks (SPNs), a class of tractable probabilistic models, to perform Bayesian model selection. Specifically, we introduce a novel approach to learn the structure of SPNs that allows for efficient marginal likelihood estimation. We demonstrate that ABDA can automatically discover high-quality density models across a wide range of datasets, often outperforming manually tuned models.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence
